title: Beauverdia felipponei

The exact classification of Beauverdia felipponei may be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy.

intro: Beauverdia felipponei, also known as "Copahu" or "Copahu resin", is a plant native to the Gran Chaco region of South America, its resin being the primary component used for medicinal purposes. The resin of Beauverdia felipponei has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues, including arthritis, joint pain, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Beauverdia felipponei resin include diterpene esters, particularly beauvericin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Beauverdia felipponei resin is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as tinctures, ointments, and creams, which are applied topically to alleviate pain and inflammation.
